card1.title: Safety first

lessons.heading: Lessons
lessons.body: Infant survival swim and toddler water-confidence sessions, scheduled in focused blocks. Full schedule and age groups are coming soon -- use contact below to ask about availability.

about.p1: Drowning is silent. It happens faster than you think. Most accidents don't look like what you imagine. No splashing. No screaming. Just seconds. And it doesn't only happen in deep water or crowded pools. It happens where kids feel safest — at home, in everyday moments, when barriers fail. That's why supervision alone isn't enough. That's why fences aren't enough.
about.p2: Survival skills aren't optional — they're essential.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.body: Answers to common questions about lessons, what to bring, and how booking works will live here.

hero.lead: Our ISR Self-Rescue programs are designed to nurture water confidence in a safe, loving environment.
hero.imgAlt: Toddler smiling in a bright pool

core.heading: Core Programs
core.lead: Essential water safety skills designed to build confidence and stay with your child for a lifetime.

infant.title: Self-Rescue® Infant
infant.description: Infants learn the essential self-rescue skill of rolling onto their back to float, rest, and breathe. They are taught to stay calm and maintain this position independently until help arrives—giving them a critical layer of safety in the water.

toddler.title: Self-Rescue® Toddler
toddler.description:
Children learn the swim-float-swim sequence, for reaching safety.
They swim with their face in the water, roll onto their back to rest and breathe.
They continue swimming until they reach the wall, steps, or edge.

continuing.heading: Continuing Education
continuing.lead: Polishing skills and building endurance through playful practice.
continuing.subtitle: Previously taught children

refresher.title: Refresher
refresher.body: 2–3 week course to sharpen skills after a break from the water.

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.body: Ongoing weekly lessons to keep muscle memory fresh and confidence high.

strokes.title: Strokes & Form
strokes.body: Introduction to formal swimming strokes and technical efficiency.

faq.heading: Common Questions
faq.lead: Everything you need to know before your first splash.

q1.q: Why is the program 5 days a week?
q1.a: Consistency is key for infant muscle memory. Short, daily exposures help build skills faster and safer than longer sessions once a week.

q2.q: How long are the actual lessons?
q2.a: Lessons are 10 minutes long. This is the optimal window for focused learning in young children without physical or mental fatigue.

q3.a: Crying is a natural form of communication. Our instructor is trained to nurture and build trust, turning tears into triumphant smiles over time.

pricingDetails.heading: Pricing details
pricingDetails.imgAlt: ISR instructor in a yellow swim shirt with a baby by the pool
pricingDetails.p1: Tuition for the core ISR program (typically 6–7 weeks of short, daily lessons) is $750 total. A 50% non-refundable deposit ($375) is required to reserve your lesson block. The remaining balance is due one day before the first day of lessons.
pricingDetails.p2: Refresher tuition is $125 per week. A $125 non-refundable deposit is required at registration. The remaining balance is due one day before the first day of lessons. Maintenance lessons are $25 per class and are paid in full at the time of registration.
pricingDetails.p3: ISR national registration: a separate non-refundable fee paid directly to Infant Swimming Resource before lessons begin. It is $105 per child for the first calendar year and $40 each following year. This covers ISR's required health and aquatic review and official lesson approval.
pricingDetails.p4: Scholarships and payment plans may be available upon request—we never want cost alone to stand between your family and water safety.

hero.heading: Cynthia, ISR Certified
hero.lead:
I'm originally from Mexico and have been building my life in Ohio with my family for the past 8 years. My journey into survival swim didn't start as a business — it started as a mom.

My son, Sebastián, changed everything for me.

As a parent of a child with unique needs, I understand the fear and the responsibility of keeping your child safe. When I discovered survival swim and saw how it empowers children — with or without special needs — I knew this was more than swimming.

It was life-saving.

That's why I became an instructor.

At WaveFront, I create a personalized and respectful environment where children learn the skills to save themselves — and parents gain something priceless: peace of mind.

Because drowning is silent.

And prevention starts with preparation.
hero.introTitle: Hi, I'm Cynthia — ISR Certified Instructor

isrOrg.heading: What is ISR?
isrOrg.body:
ISR is the global leader in the industry it pioneered in 1966: survival swimming lessons for infants and young children. Our team of highly-trained ISR Instructors provides the safest and most effective survival swimming lessons available. The ISR lessons our students receive today are a product of nearly 60 years of research and experience to achieve unparalleled results each day in pools around the world.

Today, our mission, "Not One More Child Drowns," is the foundation of everything we do and is the driving force behind ISR's employees, our independent ISR Instructors, and our major corporate partnerships. We believe the successful prevention of the leading cause of accidental death for children under the age of 4 in the U.S. will require a large group of caring and capable professionals whose sole focus is to save lives.

To date, we have delivered more than 19,000,000 ISR Self-Rescue Lessons. ISR believes in multiple layers of defense against aquatic accidents which include pool fences, alarms, and active adult supervision. However, traditional lines of defense break down, and the over 4,000 drowning deaths per year bear a grim testament to the fact that traditional approaches are missing a key component: the child. ISR's core conviction is that the child is the most important part of a drowning prevention strategy and our over 450,000 ISR graduates and hundreds of documented survival stories, are proof that children can, and do, save themselves. Children are curious, capable, and have an uncanny ability to overcome obstacles like pool fences; at ISR we take that ability and teach them skills to potentially save themselves if they find themselves in the water alone.
isrOrg.point1Text: Each child goes through ISR's required health and aquatic review and formal lesson approval before instruction starts—so skills are planned for the individual, not a generic class roster.
isrOrg.point2Text: Certified instructors complete intensive in-water training, exams, and ongoing renewal to keep their license active.
isrOrg.point3Text: The focus is survival: floating, breath control, and recovery when a child reaches the water alone.
isrOrg.footnote: WaveFront is an independent provider of the official ISR Self-Rescue program.

certs.heading: Credentials & Certifications
certs.lead: ISR sets the national bar for self-rescue instruction. Here is how Cynthia stays aligned with the organization—plus the pediatric safety training families expect.

waterSafety.heading: Water Safety
waterSafety.intro: It doesn't matter if your child knows how to swim—no one is drown-proof, and nothing replaces active adult supervision. Drowning is still the leading cause of accidental death for children ages 1–4. That's why multiple layers of protection matter... because even the most attentive supervision can fail.

supervision.title: Effective Supervision
supervision.body: Your strongest line of defense is you. No swim skill replaces active, focused supervision. If your child is ever missing—check the water first.

fences.title: Pool Fences
fences.body: Add a permanent 4-sided fence with self-latching, self-locking gates. Keep it set back a few feet from the pool (about 3–5 ft) for added safety.

locks.title: Locks & Alarms
locks.body: Secure every access point. Doors and windows leading to the pool should be locked and equipped with alarms.

cpr.title: CPR
cpr.body: Be ready for the unexpected. Parents and caregivers should know CPR for infants, children, and adults—and keep those skills up to date.

swim.title: Survival Swim Lessons
swim.body: Self-rescue skills add another layer of protection, giving your child the ability to respond in the water when it matters most.

location.body: Lessons are held at private residences in Mid Ohio.
location.cta: View schedule to learn the location for specific sessions.
